bibliography.direct_quote: We investigate the statistics of MAPE, RSME, MSE and MAD between the training data set and the validation data set to assess the overall model fit and generalization.
Moreover, we used one-sided Diebold-Mariano (DM) tests to measure incremental predictive accuracy between each sequential model.

Forecasting techniques are evaluated using four metrics: mean absolute deviation (MAD), means square error (MSE), root means squared error (RMSE) and mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) for forecast horizons h = 1, 4, 7, 14 days ahead.
The machine learning XGBoost model showed a 70% improvement over the ARIMA in the MAPE when used on a 1-day and a 30% improvement in the MAPE on a 14-day horizon.
The DM tests show that all machine learning algorithms improved the forecasts at an a = 0.05 level over the ARIMA and the Naïve forecasts for all time horizons.
